#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Gaza War Update — Mar 31, 2026

Israeli airstrikes killed at least five Palestinians on March 31 — three in Jabalia and two in Khan Younis — continuing targeted operations despite the five-month-old ceasefire, as the cumulative Gaza death toll reached 72,285. On March 30, Houthi rebels in Yemen launched a ballistic missile at Israel, the first attack since the October 2025 ceasefire began, signaling renewed Axis of Resistance pressure as Gaza diplomacy stalls. UNRWA Commissioner-General Philippe Lazzarini called for a formal UN investigation into the killing of 391 UNRWA employees, the highest staff death toll of any UN agency in any conflict in history. Hamas has still not formally responded to the Board of Peace disarmament roadmap, with Phase 2 negotiations suspended and the 2026 Flash Appeal remaining over 93% unfunded.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, kpis, casualties, political, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/gaza-war/

https://watchboard.dev/iran-conflict/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Iran Conflict Update — Mar 31, 2026

Day 32 of the Iran-US/Israel conflict. IDF/USAF struck 170+ targets in a wide-scale wave over Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, and Parchin — hitting drone engine factories, warhead casting plants, and military HQs, bringing the cumulative total to 11,000+ targets. Iran retaliated by striking the Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at the Port of Dubai while fully loaded, igniting a fire and raising oil spill fears. The US national gas price hit $4.02/gallon for the first time since 2022. Iran formally transmitted a counter-reply to Trump's 15-point peace proposal via Pakistan, rejecting US conditions as 'excessive, unrealistic and unreasonable,' while the White House said talks were 'going well.' Lebanon's government took the unprecedented step of formally banning Hezbollah's military wing and notifying the UN, as Brent crude dipped to ~$107/bbl on diplomatic optimism.

Sections updated: events, kpis, meta, casualties, econ, political, mapLines, mapPoints, claims, assets

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/iran-conflict/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mar 31, 2026

Israeli strikes killed 3 Palestinians in Gaza City's Zeitoun neighborhood on March 30 and 5 more in two attacks on March 31, with a further 3 Palestinians killed in the West Bank on Palestine Land Day's 50th anniversary — including a 15-year-old at Dheisheh refugee camp near Bethlehem. Iran launched another ballistic missile barrage on Day 31 (March 30) wounding 10 Israelis, as Human Rights Watch confirmed Iran had used cluster munitions against Israeli population centers including Yehud and Ramat Gan. The Wall Street Journal reported Trump told aides on March 31 he is willing to end the Iran war without requiring the Strait of Hormuz to reopen, while France denied Israeli use of French airspace for US weapons and Italy blocked US military aircraft at Sicily — marking the deepest NATO fracture since the war began. A CNN news team was also assaulted and detained by Israeli soldiers in the West Bank on March 31.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, mapPoints, kpis, casualties, econ, political, meta

https://watchboard.dev/mencho-cjng/

#OSINT #Security #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Mencho/CJNG Update — Mar 31, 2026

Day 37 of the post-Mencho succession crisis: O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues cementing control under a collective council structure, leveraging Grupo Élite's paramilitary capacity to enforce organizational cohesion. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains the primary unresolved high-value targeting objective with a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty. Operativo Enjambre is transitioning from reactive corruption sweeps to an institutionalized anti-corruption enforcement framework following dozens of CJNG-linked official arrests across Jalisco and Michoacán. U.S.-Mexico fentanyl interdiction operations continue despite disruption to CJNG's senior logistics tier; the cartel's port-level franchise structure has maintained precursor supply continuity.

Sections updated: events, kpis, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/mencho-cjng/

https://watchboard.dev/sudan-conflict/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 31, 2026

Sudan's civil war reached Day 1081 with the RSF-SPLM-N siege of Dilling (South Kordofan) entering its third day, Habila road severed and basic commodities depleted; the Sudan Doctors Network warned of an El Fasher-scale catastrophe. In Blue Nile, RSF-SPLM-N shifted focus south toward Geissan threatening the Roseires Dam and Ed Damazin, with 73,000+ civilians displaced into the state capital since Eid. MSF published a landmark report documenting 3,396 sexual violence survivors (97% women and girls) treated at MSF Darfur facilities between January 2024 and November 2025, calling RSF use of rape a systematic 'weapon of war.' The UN condemned escalating drone attacks on civilians in Sudan as potentially constituting war crimes, and the MSF sexual violence figure updated the tracker's casualty data.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, kpis, casualties, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/sudan-conflict/

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Taiwan Strait Tensions Tracker Update — Mar 31, 2026

PLA activity dropped sharply to 1 sortie on both March 30 and March 31 — from the 19-sortie peak on March 29 — confirming the late-March escalation as a bounded spike-and-retract signal rather than the start of a sustained campaign. A bipartisan US Senate Foreign Relations Committee delegation (Sens. Shaheen, Curtis, Rosen, Tillis) arrived in Taipei on March 30 and publicly pressed Taiwan's legislature to pass the stalled NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget. KMT Taichung Mayor Lu Shiow-yen broke with the official KMT caucus position, signaling an NT$800B–NT$1T compromise range — the first crack in KMT unity on the defense budget. Political section updated with AIT Director Raymond Greene and KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun; claims section updated to reflect the narrowing inter-party gap under sustained US pressure.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, kpis, political, claims, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/taiwan-conflict/

#Governance #Politics #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Trump Presidencies Update — Mar 31, 2026

US-Israel strikes hit Isfahan on Day 31 of the Iran war, with sustained secondary detonations indicating IRGC missile production facilities were targeted. Trump claimed 'great progress' in talks but Iran's Foreign Ministry flatly denied any direct negotiations, maintaining that all contact has been indirect via Pakistani and Turkish intermediaries. The April 6 deadline for renewed US energy infrastructure strikes remained in place. The Supreme Court ruled 8-1 in Chiles v. Salazar that Colorado's conversion therapy ban violates free speech — a win for religious conservatives aligned with the administration. BLS released January 2026 JOLTS data showing 6.946 million job openings, above forecasts, with markets bracing for the March nonfarm payrolls report on Good Friday April 3. Florida Gov. DeSantis signed legislation renaming Palm Beach International Airport for Trump pending FAA approval.

Sections updated: events, kpis, econ, claims, political, mapPoints, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/trump-presidencies/

Ukraine War Update — Mar 31, 2026

Zelensky proposed an Easter ceasefire (Orthodox Easter April 16) and a mutual halt on energy infrastructure strikes, while securing 'historic' security agreements with Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar — expanding Ukraine's diplomatic network beyond Western allies. Ukrainian forces made tactical advances near Slovyansk as Russia pushed on the Hulyaipole axis in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, with 147 combat engagements and 970 Russian losses bringing the cumulative total to ~1,297,670 on Day 1,496 of the invasion. A Ukrainian drone strike killed 1 person in Taganrog, Rostov Oblast, while falling drone debris sparked a fire at a Volgograd oil refinery; Russia intercepted 102 Ukrainian UAVs across multiple regions. Russia's daily barrage continued with 9,355 kamikaze drones deployed (including frontline FPVs), 70 airstrikes, and 237 guided aerial bombs across Ukraine.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, mapPoints, kpis, political, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/ukraine-war/
